name: Mauritania

colors.symbolism: the gold of the crescent and star, and the sands of the Sahara
the blood shed by Mauritanians who fought against French colonial rule (added in 2017)

description: A green field with a gold five-pointed star above a gold horizontal crescent (concave side up) at the centre, flanked by a narrow red horizontal stripe at the top and another at the bottom.
history: Mauritania's first national flag — a green field with a centred gold crescent and star — was adopted on 1 April 1959 ahead of independence from France in 1960. Two narrow red stripes were added at the top and bottom by constitutional referendum on 5 August 2017 to honour the sacrifice of Mauritanians who died resisting French colonisation; the new design entered force on 15 August 2017.

features.symbolism: a green field charged with a gold crescent and star — the colours of Islam — flanked by red bands added in 2017 to honour the blood shed by Mauritanians resisting French colonisation
Islam; placed within the opening of the crescent
